findings.evidence: Line 9 is the only import of `TauCeti.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.LaxMilgram` under `code/TauCeti/Analysis/PDE`; the new public theorem at lines 68-71 only states `IsCoercive (energyIntegrand ...)`, while line 72 uses `TauCeti.IsCoercive.mono` only in the proof body.
findings.fix: Make the Lax--Milgram import non-public, or move/use a smaller coercivity-monotonicity helper from a narrow public module; the perturbation theorem statements do not need to reexport the solution API.
findings.issue: `CoercivePerturbation` has `public import TauCeti.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.LaxMilgram`, so importing the perturbation file also exposes the unrelated `solutionOfInner`/`solutionOfFunctional` Lax--Milgram API.

summary: The new perturbation API itself is small, but the module reexports the entire Lax--Milgram solution layer just to use one proof helper internally. That widens the public surface beyond the named PDE perturbation target.
